title: Lastrea caucaensis
taxonomy: Lastrea caucaensis is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Lastrea, which is a part of the fern family. The classification of Lastrea caucaensis is further detailed within the family Polypodiopsida, which consists of vascular plants that reproduce via spores. This fern is native to certain regions, including parts of Central and South America. The classification of Lastrea caucaensis is also further detailed within the subfamily Dryopteridoideae.

intro: Lastrea caucaensis, also known as Cauca fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from various health issues, such as inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Lastrea caucaensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
